23.reason: [If the file "article.txt" does not exist or cannot be opened, fopen returns NULL, and subsequent fscanf on a NULL pointer causes undefined behavior (likely crash). The program does not check if file opening succeeded.]

25.reason: [fscanf with %s reads space-delimited tokens, but the problem defines a "word" as only alphabetic characters. This approach fails to correctly extract words when punctuation is attached (e.g., "Hello," becomes "Hello," which includes a comma, violating the word definition). Also, if file is NULL, this causes a crash.]

This approach fails when words are adjacent to punctuation (e.g., "hello,world" becomes one token "hello,world", which is not split correctly). Also, if file is NULL, this causes a crash.]

69.reason: [This increments count before checking if the word is truly new. Later, the word is unconditionally added again, causing double-counting or incorrect counts.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [This increments the count before checking if the word is actually new. Later, the code unconditionally copies the word into WN[numofwords] and sets num=1, which overwrites or duplicates counts. The logic for counting is inverted: it should only add a new entry if not found, not always add and then check.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [The word is added to WN[numofwords] before checking for duplicates, so duplicate words get added multiple times. The increment happens, but then a new entry is still created below, leading to double-counting and incorrect frequencies.]

Then it unconditionally copies the word into WN[numofwords] and sets count=1, overwriting any prior increment. This leads to double-counting or incorrect counts.]
